name: Brain CPR — make the 4 dead brain DB writes persist
branch: fix/brain-cpr (merged to main; deployed to production)
status: DEPLOYED but live row-proof NOT achieved — see live_verification. The write-mechanism fix is correct; rows still 0 because the brain FEATURES are not reaching their writes (a deeper cause the original task did not account for).
files: lib/persistence.js (new — safePersist(promise,label): waitUntil + never-throw + await fallback + [persist:<label>] +1 logging)
lib/reflector.js (logReflection RETURNS safePersist promise; reflect() AWAITS it)
lib/reasoning-chain.js (logChain RETURNS safePersist promise; runChain() AWAITS it)
lib/memory.js (entity extraction: best-effort non-blocking safePersist; cron-summarize move documented as the correct fix)
lib/conjecture-memory.js (recordOutcome returns an awaitable, never-rejecting promise + logs +1)
lib/loop.js (cron recordOutcome calls AWAITED; parallel Build-B added a 3rd, also awaited)
tests/BRAINCPR_LIVE_TEST.md (post-deploy chat tests + Supabase row-count checks)

what_shipped.fix1_waitUntil: safePersist via @vercel/functions waitUntil (the AI Council's low-TTFB design).
what_shipped.fix1b_fluid: vercel.json fluid:true — to give waitUntil a live instance to flush into.
what_shipped.fix2_await: Switched the in-chat writes to AWAIT through the already-awaited call chain (reflector + chain inserts are ~100ms each). safePersist still wraps with waitUntil as belt-and-suspenders.
what_shipped.reflector_scope: UNCHANGED + verified: orchestrator reflectEligible gates the reflector to general/knowledge lanes; never engine/compute/fleet.

live_verification.baseline: m8_reflections / m8_reasoning_chains / m8_entities / m8_entity_mentions ALL 0 (matches the brief).
live_verification.result: STILL 0 after multiple live chat turns — across waitUntil-only, waitUntil+Fluid, AND awaited builds.
live_verification.what_DOES_work: m8_conversations (the AWAITED write) lands every turn — proves the DB connection + saveMemory path are fine.
root_causes_found: (1) waitUntil does NOT flush from M8's legacy (req,res) Node handlers — the deferred promise never gets a request context and dies on the freeze. Fluid Compute did NOT change this. (Fixed by switching to await.)

(2) DEEPER, and the reason rows are STILL 0: the brain features are not REACHING their write calls. request_traces shows my test turns classified as RESEARCH/compute/search — for which the reflector + reasoning-chain are CORRECTLY gated OFF (scope guard working). On a confirmed intent=general turn (braincpr-gen-1), the reflector ran but produced no row and NO [persist:reflect] marker appeared in the logs.

(3) Slow free-Gemini in this environment (llm_ms 13-19s in request_traces) blows the features' tight internal budgets: reflector scoring budget = 2s (M8_REFLECT_BUDGET_MS), reasoning-chain total budget = 8s (BUDGET_MS). They time out and bail BEFORE the write. So the await fix has nothing to persist.

(4) Entity extraction runs on every turn but m8_entities has never had a row — extractor returns [] or its Gemini call exceeds the turn. (My interim 5s inline-await was reverted: it added per-turn latency without reliably landing.)
live_verification.conclusion: The original 'fire-and-forget freeze' hypothesis is REAL but only PART of the story. Build-110 correctly fixes the write mechanism, but it is NECESSARY-NOT-SUFFICIENT: rows cannot appear until the features actually reach their writes.

recommended_next_fork_for_muhammad: A. Raise the brain-feature Gemini budgets to match slow free-Gemini: M8_REFLECT_BUDGET_MS ~2s->8-10s (env var, no code) and the reasoning-chain BUDGET_MS 8s->20s (code). TRADE-OFF: turns get slower WHEN the reflector/chain run (general lane only). Cheapest path to first rows.

B. Move entity extraction into the nightly cron-summarize (council's documented follow-up). The cron handler is fully awaited, so inserts land with ZERO chat latency. Best long-term home for the heavy Gemini extraction.
C. Use a faster/paid Gemini tier (or a faster provider) for the reflector/chain scoring calls so they fit the existing budgets. Costs money; fixes latency at the root.
D. Re-run tests/BRAINCPR_LIVE_TEST.md with GENUINELY general-lane prompts (no math, no lookup) once A or C is in — that is how to actually see m8_reflections / m8_reasoning_chains go 0->>0.
tests_passed: 43/43 (tests/BRAINCPR-verify.ps1, PowerShell static mirror — host has no Node)
production_build: GREEN — dpl_BvNMiLwFmprLckML8cpp3za7kKjC (sha 268c0f9) READY, nodejs:10 (still 10 functions), @vercel/functions imports cleanly, integrated cleanly with parallel Build-B.
not_touched: Bolt fleet sync (separate MHMBOLT repo), morning brief, the 4 cron schedules. No api/ function added. Parallel fun-session files NOT staged. Parallel Build-B (lean lane) integrated via rebase, not clobbered.
honest_bottom_line: Deployed and correct, but it does NOT by itself make the brain tables fill. Pick A/B/C above to actually get rows; D is how to verify.
